triageRules.definition: Prevents public capture, media playback, access request, protected reporting, deploy, or legal gate verification from completing.
triageRules.releaseRule: No open blocking bug may remain before pilot approval or public release.

triageRules.definition: Affects a core path but has a verified workaround and does not block capture, media, reporting, or launch-gate verification.
triageRules.releaseRule: Must be reviewed by the release operator before final launch.

triageRules.definition: Usability, copy, layout, or follow-up polish that does not block a core path.
triageRules.releaseRule: May be queued if it has an owner and does not affect release acceptance.

coverageEvidence.evidence: Live form matrix and browser-flow QA cover public capture paths, submit reset, fallback handling, and inbox delivery.
Media contract, live media-source QA, and browser-flow QA cover audio, video, radio, playback mapping, and follow-up routing.
Browser-flow QA and protected operator summaries cover replay, invite, collect, and create reaction signals.
Browser-flow QA and live operator report QA cover pilot feedback capture, protected triage summaries, and private export rows.
Browser-flow QA covers private access shell capture and fallback reset.
Live operator report QA covers protected counts, summaries, readiness, exports, and secret-shaped value protection.
Release readiness and open-approvals QA keep Privacy and Terms as the remaining public-launch blockers.
Private workflow matrix, field map, selection, and integration request QA cover future handoff readiness without exposing secrets.

checks.label: Form submits resolve without frozen pages
Public buttons have a clear action, destination, or capture path
Live capture intents return accepted values and reporting evidence
Live CTA, media, playback, and reporting events return accepted values
Approved music, visual, and radio sources are playable and reportable
Approved operator reporting returns protected pilot evidence
